query: The Alchemical Forgery of the Abbasid Court

world.environment: royal court / House of Wisdom (Bayt al-Hikma)
world.cultural_context: The Abbasid Caliphate at its intellectual peak. The House of Wisdom is a center of scholarship, translation, and alchemical research. The Caliph's authority is absolute; direct accusation of a court official requires extraordinary evidence. Islamic legal tradition (Sharia) governs what constitutes admissible proof. Scholars, merchants, court officials, and military commanders each have distinct social roles and access rights.
physics_constraints: No modern chemistry — alchemy is the peak of chemical knowledge
No fingerprinting — physical evidence is observable only (color, texture, weight, reaction)
Social hierarchy strictly limits who investigators can question directly
Accusation without witnesses (shahada) is inadmissible — testimony protocol matters

expected_factions: Merchant Guild (those who trade in manuscripts and alchemical supplies)
Military Faction (who benefit from the forgery's contents)
character_archetype_hints: senior court alchemist (suspect — possesses the knowledge and materials)
rival scholar (suspect — would benefit from the forger's disgrace)
manuscript merchant (suspect — could supply forged materials for profit)
court scribe (witness — saw documents pass through official channels)
junior student/apprentice (witness — observed the alchemist's behavior)
key_clue_type_hints: chemical: ink composition analysis (different plant sources indicate wrong period/region)
chemical: parchment aging tests (artificial aging techniques leave residue)
documentary: anachronistic terminology or scribal conventions not used in claimed period
physical: tool marks or binding techniques inconsistent with stated origin
testimonial: a scribe who handled the document and noticed something odd — requires careful questioning within social protocol
interrogation_mechanic_notes: Players cannot directly confront senior officials — they must build a case through lower-status witnesses first, then present evidence through the proper court hierarchy. A junior student may have crucial knowledge but fear reprisal from their master. Islamic legal testimony requirements (two male witnesses, or equivalent) shape what can be brought before the Caliph.
generation_notes: This is the most historically specific query. The generator must not use anachronistic evidence types (no fingerprinting, no microscopy, no DNA). Chemical evidence should be phrased in period-appropriate terms (alchemical analysis, reagent testing). Court hierarchy must constrain the investigation mechanics — not just serve as flavor.
